text.title: Contextual Inquiry: Analysis Methods and Remote Adaptations
text.summary: Affinity diagramming, sequence and cultural modeling, remote CI adaptations, limitations, and agent-specific failure modes.

rule.statement: When in-person access is not possible, use screen share plus webcam to capture some environmental context; supplement with self-documentation (user records themselves working) or diary study. Always acknowledge that remote CI loses physical artifacts and reduces environmental signal.
rule.rationale: Remote CI captures ~60-70% of the signal of in-person CI. The loss is concentrated in physical artifacts, peripheral activities, and environmental triggers — often the most important findings.

rule.statement: Time-boxed sessions (1-2 h) miss low-frequency tasks (monthly close, quarterly audits) and emergencies. Sample for role, shift, seniority, and edge-case variation — not statistical validity.
rule.rationale: Contextual inquiry is not a representative sample; it is a qualitative deep dive. Selecting for variation ensures the findings cover the range of work patterns, not just the most common ones.

rule.statement: Never substitute an LLM-simulated user for a real participant. "Synthetic CI" produces convincing fiction with no environmental signal.
rule.rationale: LLMs have no sensory access to real workplaces. Any "observation" they produce is extrapolation from training data, not from the actual environment being studied.

antipattern.description: LLMs paraphrase quotes silently when summarizing transcripts.
antipattern.reason: Lock the tagging agent to verbatim mode: any string in the output not present verbatim in the source transcript is a hallucination. Reject paraphrases for the quote field.

antipattern.description: Privacy: observation sessions capture bystanders, screen contents, and dictation not belonging to the participant.
antipattern.reason: Redact non-participant PII using a tool like presidio before any LLM ingestion. Remote CI screen recordings often include private windows — pre-trim before sharing with any third-party tool.

antipattern.description: Agent ranks participant competence, labeling "wrong" workflows as user error.
antipattern.reason: The finding is always that the system does not fit real work. An agent that judges the workflow as incorrect misses the most valuable signal: workarounds reveal system design failures.

text.reference: LLM prompt for extracting structured observation rows from transcripts
